event: Admitted for induction chemotherapy with cytarabine and clofarabine
Developed loose stools and diffuse abdominal pain
Found to have Clostridium difficile infection
Started oral metronidazole treatment
Abdominal pain persisted and localized to the right lower quadrant
Neutropenic but remained afebrile
Abdominal and pelvic CT scan showed a segmental hypoenhancing area in the mid appendix with minimal surrounding fat stranding
Treated with broad-spectrum intravenous antibiotics consisting of meropenem
Remained afebrile and hemodynamically normal on medical treatment
Developed localized peritoneal signs
Repeat CT scan showed stable inflammation of the appendix with a new finding of an adjacent loop of small bowel with thickened wall
Underwent urgent appendectomy for treatment of acute appendicitis
Found to be completely necrotic down to the base
Converted to open surgery to perform an ileocecectomy with primary stapled anastomosis
Admitted to the intensive care unit postoperatively
Extubated on postoperative day 1
Spiked a fever to 38.5°C on postoperative day 2
Clinically deteriorated on postoperative day 4 and re-intubated for tachypnea and hypoxia
CT scan of the chest showed numerous peripheral cavitary lesions
Bronchoalveolar lavage was performed to aid diagnosis
Pathological diagnosis of zygomycosis was made from the ileocecectomy specimen
Fungal overgrowth was found in the surgical wound
Culture of the bronchoalveolar lavage recovered Absidia spp.
Died on postoperative day 8
